calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment, Discharge Forfeiture And Exonerate Bail
Off Calendar Jul-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Deputy City Attorney, Diana Rosenstein (415-554-3933), appears for plaintiff/opposing party, the People of the State of California

Attorney, John M. Rorabaugh (714-617-9600), appears telephonically for defendant/moving party, Financial Casualty & Surety

The matter is argued by both sides. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FINANCIAL CASUALTY & SURETY, INC.'S motion to set aside summary judgment, discharge forfeiture and exonerate bail is GRANTED. Ms. Evans' statements on September 26, 2014 that "I have been in touch with Mr. Shan since the last calling of this case. I think there may be some confusion as when he is supposed to come back to court" are an insufficient basis for a judge to have a reason to believe that there was a sufficient excuse may exist for Mr. Shan's non-appearance in court that day. There is no explanation about what the confusion might be or why it was not cleared up by Ms. Evans. The statements by Ms. Evans fail to disclose any ground to believe that Mr. Shan's non-appearance was due to any possible justifiable basis. A written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (D302)
